message:
feat(robot): expand robot-mode detection and suppress duplicate structured errors

Two related robot-mode / structured-output improvements driven by
downstream tooling that kept getting double error payloads and
missed --robot-format invocations.

1. Wider robot-mode detection in `is_robot_mode_args`
   Previously only bare `--json`, `--robot`, `-json`, `-robot` flipped
   the binary into robot mode. Now also honours:
     - `--robot-format <fmt>` and `--robot-format=<fmt>` (any position,
       any value — bitmap match is intentional: an unknown format still
       indicates robot intent and errors should surface structured)
     - `CASS_OUTPUT_FORMAT` env var when set to a recognised format
       (`json`, `jsonl`, `compact`, `sessions`, `toon`)
     - `TOON_DEFAULT_FORMAT` env var when set to `json` or `toon` —
       kept separate because TOON is an independent toggle
   Factored the env-var side into `env_requests_robot_output()` and
   made the arg loop a conventional `for` so the new `starts_with`
   check has a clean home.

2. Suppress duplicate structured-format error payloads
   `run_health`, `run_doctor`, and `run_index_with_data` previously
   wrote a structured error payload to stdout and then returned the
   same `CliError` to the caller, which wrote the payload again (once
   pretty for humans, once structured for robots — or in the worst
   case, twice on stderr). Callers observing both streams saw two
   JSON documents for one failure.
   - All three now thread the final error through
     `CliError::already_reported_from(&err)` whenever
     `structured_format.is_some()`, so stdout gets exactly one
     payload and stderr stays empty for a successful
     already-serialised error.
   - The existing pretty/human path is unchanged.

3. Consolidate `run_index_with_data` event-stream setup
   - Moved `env_disabled`/`emit_progress_events`/`progress_interval`
     computation above the `active_index_run_details` pre-flight.
   - Active-index pre-flight failures now emit an `error` NDJSON
     event on stderr *before* writing the structured payload to stdout,
     so event-stream consumers see a coherent `started → error`
     sequence instead of silence.
   - Simplified `emit_event` to take only the payload (the event name
     is already embedded in `payload["event"]`), removing a dead
     `let _ = event` and a confusing dual-argument signature.

4. Test harness: `run_on_large_stack`
   `include_attachments_still_accepted_when_explicitly_passed` runs
   clap's derive-generated parser, which blows the default 2 MiB
   thread stack in debug builds because of the monomorphised dispatch.
   Added a small `run_on_large_stack` helper that spawns a 16 MiB
   stack thread and pins the test there, so it passes without needing
   the outer runner to have set `RUST_MIN_STACK`.

Coverage in tests/cli_robot.rs:
- `search_missing_index_returns_json_error_contract_with_robot_format_compact`
  asserts `--robot-format compact` participates in the missing-index
  JSON error contract.
- `search_missing_index_returns_json_error_contract_with_env_output_format`
  does the same for `CASS_OUTPUT_FORMAT=compact`.
- Extended `status_missing_db_reports_not_initialized` and
  `health_missing_db_reports_not_initialized` to assert semantic
  status is `not_initialized` (not "failed") on fresh installs and
  that `health --json` does not double-emit on stderr.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
